title: Silent OSError handling hides file access failures
description:
## Location
scripts/analyze-hetero-fleet.py:54

## Description
When read_text() raises OSError, the exception is caught and the file is silently skipped via 'continue'. No warning is logged. Users won't know why a node is missing from the report, especially if the file exists but has permission issues.

## Failure Scenario
A log file py-1000.log exists but has mode 000 (no read permission). The OSError is caught and silently ignored. The final report shows 0 Python nodes, and the user assumes no Python nodes participated in the test, when actually the data exists but couldn't be read.

## Category
error-handling
